pick.rationale: Vanderbilt holds a significant analytical edge with KenPom rank 12 and Torvik rank 10 compared to McNeese's KenPom rank 67, featuring elite offensive efficiency (rank 6) that should exploit the Cowboys' weaker defensive profile. While McNeese enters on a 10-game winning streak and has tournament experience with three consecutive March Madness appearances, their 0-2 record against Quad 1 opponents and 201st-ranked strength of schedule suggest they haven't faced a team of Vanderbilt's caliber. The Commodores' SEC-tested talent and 11.5-point favoritism reflect the substantial gap in this classic 5/12 matchup, making Vanderbilt the clear choice despite the upset potential inherent in this seeding.

reasoningStep.title: Vanderbilt vs McNeese 5/12 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Vanderbilt's superior analytical profile and strength of schedule give them a decisive edge over a McNeese team that lacks quality wins despite strong recent form.

result.text: McNeese vs. Vanderbilt (Mar 19, 2026) Pregame - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Vanderbilt Commodores vs McNeese Cowboys NCAA Men's Basketball Championship - South Region - 1st Round 12 McNeese Cowboys McNeese MCN 28-5 VAN -12.5 truTV 5 Vanderbilt Commodores Vanderbilt VAN 26-8 Gamecast Preview Videos Bracket Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge Vanderbilt faces McNeese in opening round of NCAA Tournament By Data Skrive Mar 18, 2027, 04:46 am Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email McNeese Cowboys (28-5, 21-3 Southland) vs. Vanderbilt Commodores (26-8, 13-8 SEC) Oklahoma City; Thursday, 3:15 p.m. EDT Commodores -11.5; over/under is 150.5 BOTTOM LINE: No. 16 Vanderbilt plays in the first round of the NCAA Tournament against McNeese. The Commodores are 13-8 against SEC opponents and 13-0 in non-conference play. Vanderbilt is 2-2 in one-possession games. The Cowboys' record in Southland action is 21-3. McNeese is the best team in the Southland scoring 16.0 fast break points per game. Vanderbilt makes 47.5% of its shots from the field this season, which is 6.8 percentage points higher than McNeese has allowed to its opponents (40.7%). McNeese averages 80.0 points per game, 4.8 more than the 75.2 Vanderbilt gives up to opponents. TOP PERFORMERS: Tyler is averaging 19.1 points, 5.1 assists and 2.4 steals for the Commodores. Duke Miles is averaging 13.1 points and 3.0 rebounds while shooting 42.6% over the past 10 games. Larry Johnson is scoring 17.5 points per game and averaging 5.5 rebounds for the Cowboys. Tyshawn Archie is averaging 2.3 made 3-pointers over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Commodores: 6-4, averaging 80.8 points, 30.6 rebounds, 14.5 assists, 6.8 steals and 3.4 blocks per game while shooting 45.0% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 78.6 points per game. Cowboys: 10-0, averaging 77.2 points, 30.1 rebounds, 13.8 assists, 10.8 steals and 3.7 blocks per game while shooting 46.1% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 64.0 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar .
